DISCOVERY 612, PLAINTIFF THELEN REID BROWN RAYMAN & STEINER'S MOTION TO COMPEL FURTHER RESPONSES TO FORM INTERROGATORIES, SET ONE; FURTHER RESPONSES TO DEMAND FOR INSPECTION, PRODUCTION AND COPYING OF DOCUMENTS, SET ONE, IS MOOT IN PART AND GRANTED IN PART. THE MOTION HAS BEEN WITHDRAWN AS IT SEEKS VERIFICATIONS. AS THE REQUESTS FOR PRODUCTION HAVE RESPONSES WHICH INCORPORATE ALL OBJECTIONS AS MADE TO THE FORM INTERROGATORIES, THOSE OBJECTIONS ARE OVERRULED AND ORDERED STRICKEN. THE RESPONSES OTHERWISE MAY BE ADEQUATE. AS TO FORM INTERROGATORY NOS. 9.1, 12.1, AND 14.1, THE OBJECTIONS ARE OVERRULED AND ORDERED STRICKEN. THE CODE OF CIVIL PROCEDURE DOES NOT PROVIDE FOR A RESERVATION OF A RIGHT TO SUPPLEMENT A RESPONSE. AS TO FORM INTERROGATORY NOS. 9.2 AND 17.1, THE OBJECTIONS ARE OVERRULED AND ORDERED STRICKEN. THE RESPONSES ARE INADEQUATE AS THEY DO NOT IDENTIFY THE RESPONSIVE DOCUMENTS WITH PARTICULARITY. AS TO FORM INTERROGATORY NOS. 12.2 AND 12.3, THE OBJECTIONS ARE OVERRULED AND ORDERED STRICKEN. THE RESPONSES OTHERWISE MAY BE ADEQUATE. AS TO FORM INTERROGATORY NOS. 14.1 AND 15.1, THE OBJECTIONS ARE OVERRULED AND ORDERED STRICKEN. THE RESPONSES ARE INADEQUATE, AND DEFENDANT IS ORDERED TO RESPOND FURTHER. DEFENDANT IS ORDERED TO RESPOND FURTHER WITHIN 15 DAYS OF NOTICE OF THIS ORDER. THE COURT FINDS THAT THE RESPONSES WERE A MISUSE OF THE DISCOVERY PROCESS. SANCTIONS ARE AWARDED AGAINST DEFENDANT IN THE AMOUNT OF $2,015, TO BE PAID WITHIN 30 DAYS OF NOTICE OF THIS ORDER. ORDER SIGNED IN OPEN COURT. COMM: EVERETT A. HEWLETT

Notice Of Motion And Motion To Compel Further Response To Form Interrogatories, Set One, Further Responses To Demand For Inspection, Production And Copying Of Documents, Set One, And To Provide Verifications In Proper Format; Request For Sanctions
Off Calendar Oct-12-2007 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: APPEARANCES: ROGER T. RITTER, ESQ. (415)433-5333, FOR PLAINTIFF/MOVING PARTY
RICHARD M. OSBORNE, ESQ. (925)335-2344, FOR DEFENDANT/OPPOSING PARTY

TENTATIVE RULING ADOPTED. Plaintiff Thelen Reid Brown Rayman & Steiner's Motion To Compel Further Responses To Form Interrogatories, Set one; Further Responses To Demand For Inspection, Production And Copying Of Documents, Set One, is moot in part and granted in part. The motion has been withdrawn as it seeks verifications. As the Requests for Production have responses which incorporate all objections as made to the Form Interrogatories, those objections are overruled and ordered stricken. The responses otherwise may be adequate. As to Form Interrogatory Nos. 9.1, 12.1, and 14.1, the objections are overruled and ordered stricken. The Code of Civil Procedure does not provide for a reservation of a right to supplement a response. As to Form Interrogatory Nos. 9.2 and 17.1, the objections are overruled and ordered stricken. The responses are inadequate as they do not identify the responsive documents with particularity. As to Form Interrogatory Nos. 12.2 and 12.3, the objections are overruled and ordered stricken. The responses otherwise may be adequate. As to Form Interrogatory Nos. 14.1 and 15.1, the objections are overruled and ordered stricken. The responses are inadequate, and Defendant is ordered to respond further.
Defendant is ordered to respond further within 15 days of notice of this order. The court finds that the responses were a misuse of the discovery process. Sanctions are awarded against Defendant in the amount of $2,015, to be paid within 30 days of notice of this order. ORDER SIGNED IN OPEN COURT. COMM: EVERETT A. HEWLETT
